body_txt: A Boss Battle where the boss remains in one place. In side-scrolling games, this typically means the left or right side of the screen, though in 3D games, the distinction gets a lot blurrier. It might be because the boss is too big to move and be a fair fight or because it's attached to and/or merged with something and can't move. In older games, large bosses were largely stationary due to sprite limit count (and having to use background tiles as an alternative) and the difficulty of animating background tiles note especially dynamic animations of the sort that would be expected from active entities like a boss, as opposed to short loops of stuff like trees blowing in the wind or pulsating fleshy walls that a simplistic background could get away with (and probably not even that in the limitations of 8-bit or crude/early 16-bit) . A boss that stands in the background and launches attacks into the foreground is a Background Boss . If the boss remains on one side of the screen and is edging ever closer, requiring you to defeat him while running away, he may also be an Advancing Boss of Doom note which technically isn't stationary , since the boss is moving , but it still counts as this trope if the screen scrolls at the same pace as the boss's movement so that the boss stays in the same place relative to everything else on the screen . Stationary Enemy is this but for regular enemies. Compare Pivotal Boss , for when the boss is a physical pivot for the battle to literally turn around, and Battleship Raid , where a boss isn't just big, but forms the focus of an entire section of the level, playing the part of both scenery and boss. Contrast High-Speed Battle . Not to be confused with a Stationery Boss who attacks you with memos, box files, pens, ink, staplers, etc.
